name: Ascent to the Santuario Virgen de la Cabeza — circular route through the Parque Natural Sierra de Andújar
name_original: Subida al Santuario Virgen de la Cabeza — circular por el Parque Natural Sierra de Andújar
region_intro: Road cycling around Andújar offers diverse terrain within the Sierra Morena, characterized by undulating hills and more rugged sections, particularly along river courses. The region features extensive Mediterranean forests of holm oaks and cork oaks, providing scenic routes. River valleys, such as those of the Jándula and Yeguas, carve through the landscape, creating varied elevation profiles for road cyclists. The geological composition of shales, quartzites, and granite contributes to the varied terrain. Best road cycling routes around Andújar The most popular road cycling route is Ascent to the Santuario Virgen de la Cabeza — circular route through the Parque Natural Sierra de Andújar, a 46.7 miles (75.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Andujar - tour around Puerto de La Cadena - Parque Natural Sierra de Andujar, a moderate 25.1 miles (40.4 km) path. This route explores the natural park with varied terrain and scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the Yeguas Reservoir Loop from Andújar, a 36.1 miles (58.1 km) trail leading through the Sierra Morena region, often completed in about 2 hours 28 minutes. Road cycling around Andújar is defined by undulating hills, Mediterranean forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging climbs. The routes in Andújar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from 10 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Andújar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Andújar?
A: Road cycling around Andújar offers diverse terrain, primarily within the Sierra Morena. You'll encounter undulating hills and more rugged sections, especially along river courses like the Jándula and Yeguas. The geological composition of shales, quartzites, and granite contributes to varied elevation profiles, making for engaging rides.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Andújar area?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes around Andújar, catering to various skill levels. This includes 4 easy routes, 22 moderate routes, and 21 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Andújar?
A: Yes, Andújar offers 4 easy road cycling routes. These are ideal for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride through the scenic landscapes.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1273132029/ascent-to-the-santuario-virgen-de-la-cabeza-circular-route-through-the-parque-natural-sierra-de-andujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Santuario Virgen de la Cabeza — circular route through the Parque Natural Sierra de Andújar&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain, climbing over 1,150 meters. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996243350/andujar-and-the-campina-de-jaen-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andújar and the Campiña de Jaén Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which features nearly 1,200 meters of ascent over its 110-kilometer distance.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Andújar?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Andújar are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016756573/yeguas-reservoir-loop-from-andujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yeguas Reservoir Loop from Andújar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 58-kilometer circular route exploring the scenic Sierra Morena region. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996084181/la-campina-de-jaen-loop-from-andujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Campiña de Jaén Loop from Andújar&lt;/a&gt;, an 83.8-kilometer circular route.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass through the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park, featuring extensive Mediterranean forests of holm oaks and cork oaks. You might encounter scenic river valleys, and some routes offer views of reservoirs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2862464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encinarejo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4363470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polar Viewpoint – Aldea del Cerezo&lt;/a&gt; also offers stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1273132029/ascent-to-the-santuario-virgen-de-la-cabeza-circular-route-through-the-parque-natural-sierra-de-andujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario Virgen de la Cabeza&lt;/a&gt; is a significant cultural attraction located within the natural park, reachable by a challenging road cycling route. Additionally, you can find historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3069525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Batanera Rock Art Site&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3169663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaescrita Cave Paintings&lt;/a&gt; near some routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Andújar?
A: Andújar's Mediterranean climate makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures. Summers can be hot, while winters are generally mild. The region's clear skies, designated a Starlight Reserve, also make for beautiful evening rides or stargazing opportunities.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Andújar?
A: The road cycling routes in Andújar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the beauty of the Sierra Morena Natural Park, and the engaging challenges offered by the elevation gains.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by water features like waterfalls or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some routes offer views of water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016756573/yeguas-reservoir-loop-from-andujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yeguas Reservoir Loop from Andújar&lt;/a&gt; directly explores the scenic Sierra Morena region with its reservoir. You can also find natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Lastras&lt;/a&gt; nearby, which might be incorporated into a ride or visited separately.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available in Andújar?
A: One of the longest routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996243350/andujar-and-the-campina-de-jaen-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andújar and the Campiña de Jaén Loop&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 110 kilometers. This difficult route provides an extensive exploration of the region's diverse landscapes.
tips: This viewpoint, located in the municipality of Andújar, is one of those dedicated to pilgrims heading to the Basilica of the Virgen de la Cabeza. There is also a sculpture that represents a father and a son pointing the direction of the sanctuary.
The Basilica of Nuestra Señora de la Cabeza, located on a promontory from which you can see the Sierra de Andújar, is the second most visited Andalusian sanctuary after the hermitage of El Rocío.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Cabeza
